specific_mechanism: Hyporheic exchange: a stream bed's porous sediment is a bidirectional transit zone where surface water and groundwater mix continuously. Downwelling at one location and upwelling at another, with residence times from minutes to weeks. During transit through the hyporheic zone, water is biogeochemically transformed (oxygen depletion, nutrient processing, microbial reactions). Distinct from groundwater (unidirectional, extended scales) by being bidirectional and centimeter-to-decameter scale.
llm_application: Model the working-memory ↔ long-term-memory boundary not as a hard tier interface but as a hyporheic-style transit zone where bidirectional flow happens continuously and information is transformed during transit (compression on consolidation flow, retrieval-rich annotation on retrieval flow). The transit zone has its own residence-time distribution, transformation pathways, and dynamics; it is a first-class architectural layer rather than a thin interface.
novelty_claim: Multi-tier LLM agent memory is heavily covered: Collaborative Memory (2505.18279) has two-tier memory with read/write policies; ByteRover (2604.01599) is hierarchical context with LLM-curation; MemVerse (2512.03627) transforms raw experiences into structured long-term memories; A-Mem (2502.12110) uses Zettelkasten dynamic linking; MemGPT (dual-tier RAM-disk). All have boundaries-and-transformations but treat the boundary as a thin policy interface, not a residence-time-aware transit-zone layer with bidirectional fluxes. The hyporheic-zone-as-first-class-transit-layer framing isn't directly imported.
